title: Data Sources
description: Data Sources manages the connections between Parthenon and the OMOP CDM databases available within your deployment. Administrators and data engineers can register new sources, configure connection credentials, assign vocabulary schemas, and control which user roles have access to each source for analytical and exploratory purposes.
tips: Always assign the correct CDM version when registering a new source — mismatches between the registered version and the actual schema will cause query errors in downstream analyses.
Use descriptive, standardized source names and keycodes so that users can easily identify databases by institution, data type, and refresh date.
Restrict access to sensitive data sources at the role level rather than sharing credentials directly — use Parthenon's role-based access controls to manage authorization.
After registering a new source, run a characterization or Data Explorer summary to validate that the connection is active and the CDM tables are populated as expected.
Schedule periodic connection health checks through the System Health screen to detect database availability issues before they impact running analyses.
